refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency that ensures safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is mostly atomic, focusing on teleoperation latency and its compatibility with safety. The phrase 'compatible with safe remote operation' is kept to emphasize the safety aspect without specifying how latency is achieved or measured, maintaining solution neutrality. The FR does not contain multiple obligations requiring splitting.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the end-to-end communication latency during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify that it meets the safety criteria defined by stakeholders, such as latency p95 ≤ 150 ms.

candidate_dps: Implement a communication protocol optimized for low-latency end-to-end data transfer
Design a system architecture that prioritizes real-time data processing and minimal buffering
Incorporate adaptive data compression and prioritization to reduce transmission delays
Establish a dedicated communication channel with guaranteed bandwidth and quality of service
Implement local predictive control algorithms to compensate for communication latency
selected_dp.selected_dp: Design a system architecture that prioritizes real-time data processing and minimal buffering

coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on system architecture to minimize latency independently from specific communication protocols or hardware, supporting modularity and independence from other DPs.

assumptions_risks: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters to meet latency requirements
Operational policies do not impose additional latency constraints
Predictive control or buffering strategies are not required beyond minimal buffering
Latency measurement and monitoring mechanisms are in place to verify compliance
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including latency monitoring and adaptive control mechanisms in the DP to handle variability in network conditions and ensure consistent safe teleoperation.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the core system architecture responsible for real-time data processing and minimal buffering, integrating necessary components and managing data flows.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Bi-directional data stream for real-time telemetry data exchange.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Network communication interface for data transmission and reception.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as NGHE Solution is the core system handling data processing; minimal diffusion as most processing is internal.
Vehicle module handles physical operations; real-time data processing is peripheral, causing moderate diffusion.
Teleoperation involves remote control which requires some real-time data, but not full processing; moderate containment and diffusion.
Safety Services focus on alerts and safety logic, not primary real-time data processing; low containment, high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for containing the DP of real-time data processing with minimal buffering, as it centralizes processing logic and data flow management, minimizing diffusion across other modules.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Teleoperation for some real-time data handling may cause interface complexity.
If Vehicle module requires direct access to raw data streams, some diffusion may occur.
Safety Services may need timely data for alerts, requiring careful interface design to avoid diffusion.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Refine the DP to explicitly separate real-time data processing core functions from teleoperation and safety alert functions to improve modular containment and reduce cross-module diffusion.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP logically addresses the FR by focusing on a system architecture that prioritizes real-time data processing and minimal buffering, which directly supports achieving the teleoperation latency requirement (p95 ≤ 150 ms) for safe remote operation.

diffusion_risks: Potential overlap with Teleoperation module for some real-time data handling causing interface complexity.
Vehicle module's peripheral real-time data processing may cause moderate diffusion if it accesses raw data streams directly.
Safety Services requiring timely data for alerts may increase diffusion if interfaces are not well designed.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion and minimal diffusion, making it an appropriate IM boundary for the DP. Other modules have moderate to high diffusion scores, indicating some risk of scattered responsibilities, but overall containment is acceptable with careful interface management.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible by measuring end-to-end teleoperation latency (p95 ≤ 150 ms) under agreed network conditions and ensuring compliance with worksite safety plans and operational policies.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the FR to the NGHE Solution IM boundary is clear, as it centralizes the core real-time data processing functions necessary to meet the latency and safety requirements.

recommended_revisions: Refine the DP to explicitly separate core real-time data processing functions from teleoperation and safety alert functions to improve modular containment and reduce cross-module diffusion.

decision.justification: Although all gates have passed, the recommended revisions highlight the need to refine the DP to explicitly separate core real-time data processing from teleoperation and safety alert functions. This refinement is necessary to reduce cross-module diffusion risks and improve modular containment, which is critical given the high safety criticality of the system. Therefore, it is prudent to revise the current level before proceeding to derive next-level sub-FRs to ensure a robust and well-contained design.

revise.why: To explicitly separate core real-time data processing functions from teleoperation and safety alert functions, thereby improving modular containment and reducing cross-module diffusion risks.

assumptions: The current DP sufficiently addresses the FR logically but has modular containment issues.
High safety criticality demands minimal diffusion and clear module boundaries.
open_risks: Potential interface complexity due to overlapping real-time data handling across modules.
Moderate diffusion risk from vehicle module accessing raw data streams.
Safety services may increase diffusion if interfaces are not well designed.
evidence_needed: Refined DP design showing clear separation of core real-time processing, teleoperation, and safety alert functions.
Interface definitions demonstrating reduced diffusion and improved containment.
